entry.title: Re: I have a requirement to run the iflow on 2 specific d...
entry.content:
Hi @km4 ,

I suppose your iFlow would need to have a timer flow step, looking like an analog clock face in the diagram. If it does (or if you can add this step to your iFlow ), tab 'Scheduler' should allow you to determine the start day(s) and time(s). See (e.g.) this blog for further insights: SAP Integration Suite – Advanced Scheduler Configu... - SAP Community

entry.title: I want to send only today's files to sftp using sftp sender adapter which polls every 10mins
Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Great stuff.

Is there a release date for the SAP SuccessFactors Learning connection ?

entry.title: Re: Create a Dynamic Recipient Report based on Personalis...
entry.content:
Can do it in Business objects Design studio:

Create the two pages as described. Page 1 will show the branch name and branch manager, and Page 2 will display branch-specific sales figures.
Bind your data sources to the respective components (charts, crosstabs, etc.).

Use scripting within Design Studio to control the visibility of data. You can employ the USER() function to retrieve the current user's information and dynamically filter data based on this.

This script would be attached to the data source or component on Page 2 to ensure that branch managers only see their branch's data.

code:

var currentUser = USER();
DS_1.setFilter("BRANCH", "BRANCH_NAME = '" + currentUser + "'");
Note: Adjust the filtering logic to match your data model and user attribute configuration. Ensure the field used in the setFilter method matches the one in your data source that identifies the branch.

entry.title: Re: I want to send only today's files to sftp using sftp sender adapter which polls every 10mins
entry.content: If you only want to send files for today, then why not poll once a day instead of every 10 minutes?

entry.title: Re: I want to send only today's files to sftp using sftp...
entry.content: Hi, its polling and continuously sending all previous files as they are not letting us delete/archive them, My main concern is to only send file which has today's timestamp without modifying it. How can I achieve it

entry.title: Re: Step by Step instruction to Setup / Update IAS User Passwords in Mass using Postman
entry.content:
Thanks Yash!

How to add password as variable if you can advise please.

entry.title: Re: I want to send only today's files to sftp using sftp sender adapter which polls every 10mins
entry.content: You would need a regular expression based on the file naming format that would include the current date, so you would need to handle it with the combination of poll enrich and a start timer.

entry.title: Purchasing unit not being respected when converting Purchase Requisitions from PPDS
entry.content:
Our company uses alternate units of measure for purchasing that relate to the package type (drum, tote, etc.). These are all maintained in the purchasing tab of the material master, and in the PIR as the purchasing units that should be reflected on the Purchase Orders that we send internally as Stock Transports. When converting Purchase Requisitions manually in ME21N, these purchasing units work just fine, however, if purchase requisitions are converted via PPDS (product view), the quantity of the purchase order is always reflected as the base unit of measure.

Any ideas on how to fix this?

For context, we are on

entry.title: Lead Management: Create Lead only with contact and without "Existing Account" or "Company"
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We have a requirement to create Leads without an existing Account/Company, but it seems this is not possible.

While it's possible to create Opportunities using just a Contact, the Account is mandatory for Leads and cannot be unchecked in FineTuning / Leads / Involved Parties.

Additionally, there is no option in Scoping to create Leads without an existing Account/Company, unlike with Opportunities.

What can we do to address this requirement?

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Thanks for Information

entry.title: Request for copy of Production environment replacing current dev environment
entry.content:
We intend to copy the Current Production tenant : URL to replace current Dev environment URL

We intend to have all front end as well as backend copied along with all configurations.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request for copy of Production environment replacing...
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP Cloud for Customer offers the option to create new tenants copying all or certain data from existing tenants.

As there are some constraints in the process, we suggest you to check on KBA 2136036 - How to Request a New Tenant for SAP Business ByDesign or SAP Cloud for Customer which documents the scenario in more details.

Kind regards,
Leonardo
SAP Support
Community:
SAP Business ByDesign: https://www.sap.com/community/topics/business-bydesign.html
SAP Cloud for Customer: https://www.sap.com/community/topics/cloud-for-customer.html

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori adoption enabled by Security Role Design
entry.content:
Thanks for the blog.

We are moving users slowly from SAP GUI to Fiori, but they will still have access to SAP GUI login for a while. 

In this case, should we keep granular authorizations both in backend roles and new Fiori roles? or do the authorizations embedded in Fiori roles apply to the SAP GUI backend as well?

What is the best-practice for moving users to Fiori:

1. Do we create brand new business roles that have Catalogs/Spaces as well as granular authorizations (ie. by company code, sales division, etc.), and just not use the existing GUI roles. Or:

2. Should we keep those auths in the backend roles and just have catalogs/spaces in the new business roles? 

3. Should we add the Fiori catalogs/spaces to existing backend roles?

Not clear what the right direction is.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: New to the SAP Community? A friend of someone already here?
entry.content: Thanks for this Info

Best Regards,

Julio

In Step8, I first executed the command as-is (`sh ~/sap/hdbclient/hdbsql -U myDBAdmin`), and got an error:

After reviewing the screenshot below the instructions, I realized that 'sh' was not required.

entry.title: Re: odata Basic authorization Sample java source code?
entry.content:
Update: it works for http Basic authorization only. 

My issue is actually about Https certification.

entry.title: Re: OData development & Registration on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi @CheriyanSam,

If my memory doesn't betray me, the service "OData Provisioning" was only available on Neo - which is being discontinued. And it was targeting customers that didn't have a SAP Gateway (FES) system running on-prem.

If you are moving to the cloud, it is most likely that you are going for an S/4HANA Private edition. In that case, the private edition already comes with the means for you to publish OData services without the need of a SAP Gateway system. It all runs on the same box. 

However, to comply with the ABAP Cloud guidelines (https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/abap-cloud/ba-p/13557273) you will have to rely on published APIs and CDS Views in order to "keep the core clean". 

This means that you will have two options:

1) Keep the developed OData services the way they are during the migration - and later you will have to figure out how to move them to the new ABAP Cloud guidelines. 

2) User ABAP Cloud guidelines to "repurpose" the OData services by adhering to the published APIs and exposed CDS Views. If non are available, use BTP to develop your OData service by consuming other standard APIs in your S/4HANA system. Adopt new ways of thinking about how you do your data integration with other systems, like using the Event-Driven Architecture - which allows you to capture ERP's events and send them to a queue for later processing in BTP. 

You can easily develop new OData services on BTP using CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model). This will allow you to leverage Java and NodeJS knowledge together with CDS syntax to easily define services based on local data (cloud database) or using Cloud SDK. This SDK allows you to easily consume any S/4HANA published API and it is fully integrated with BTP features like destinations.

Hope this helps.
Ivan

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud Credit Management VKM4/VKM5 alternat...
entry.content:
Hi dear Colleague,

I recommend you to have a look on the documentation below, it will help you to find a better way to treat you needs.

Configuration for SAP Credit Management | SAP Help Portal
>> Approval Process for Documented Credit Decisions
>> Situation Handling in SAP Credit Management

Also in the customer master data its possible to see the list of a documents with credit block.

I hope it helps you on your journey for the better solution.

Regards,
Julio C.

entry.title: Re: Returning table in classes
entry.content:
Hi!

I have a similar issue working with a method that called a function....I have already put the returning table as you said, but it didn't work 😪, could you help me? Please 

 

 method ZGET_DETALLE.
*    TYPES it_detalle TYPE ty_det.
     DATA: t_det TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zccdetalle,
        l_detalle LIKE LINE  OF t_det .

    CALL FUNCTION 'ZF_DATOS_CLIENTE_LVAA'
      EXPORTING
        pncliente =  PNCLIENTE                " ELEMENTO DE LOS DATOS DEL CLIENTE
      IMPORTING
        pmensaje  = PMENSAJE                 " ELEMENTO DE DATO PARA MENSAJE*S
      TABLES
        ptcte     =  t_det             " ESTRUCTURA DETALLE CLIENTE
      .
    "PTDET[] = it_detalle[].
     IF sy-subrc = 0.
      ptdet[] = t_det[].
     ENDIF.

  CLEAR: l_detalle,t_det[].

  endmethod.

 

 In the interface

When I run the method appears this window

Obrigado novamente.

#NF-e Technical 2023.004 v1.10

#<IndDeduzDeson>

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2024 highlights on UX, application development, and AI
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire 2024 was amazing. It was definitely the year of AI. In addition to the usual executive keynotes, customer presentations, and inspirational speakers, every -focused session I attended included some discussion on how artificial intelligence will make the product experience better. Since my focus was on meeting customers and partners to explain our UX strategy and how to implement the SAP Fiori user experience as they transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, this summary will focus on those dimensions. You can see the full session catalog if you want to see what else was at the event, which also includes the ASUG Annual Conference.

UX Engineering solutions appeared across SAP Sapphire

It was extremely gratifying to see the solutions that our Cloud ERP UX Engineering team develops in so many sessions. My Home was in several keynotes, along with examples of SAP Fiori elements apps. The new SAP Fiori tools AI was in the SAP Build Code sessions and in the public cloud roadmap session. One of the most satisfying parts of the conference was having a gentleman introduce himself during one of the ASUG sessions on SAP S/4HANA migration. We had met at Sapphire around eight years ago and as a result of our conversation, he implemented SAP Screen Personas at his gourmet food company and it now runs in 15 factories, saving them lots of money every year.

The theme of SAP Sapphire 2024 was “Bring out your best.”

 

Unlock business agility with cloud ERP by adopting innovation more quickly

The first session I attended after the AI-heavy keynote was “Unlock business agility with cloud ERP”. Jan Gilg and Eric van Rossum discussed the evolution of ERP over the past decade. It continued the AI theme with examples of AI-driven insights and predictions of how they will change how customers use ERP to deliver new experiences to create new revenue streams. Eric also talked about different kinds of risk and how the risk of doing nothing is getting more expensive, as early adopters of AI will generate an insurmountable lead over their competitors.

One of the highlights was the demo of a new integration with Microsoft Copilot. They showed an intelligent, AI-based assistant that you can interact with via chat to locate any information in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. Ask questions in natural language and receive answers as text or charts, along with the ability to open the relevant app that contains the source data. It builds on the existing integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud and Microsoft Teams that enables our collaborative ERP. This enhancement adds Microsoft Teams chat with an AI assistant in addition to the ability to chat with your human colleagues.

One topic in the unlock business agility session was how much the user experience improved in the last ten years.

Watch ERP169 “Unlock business agility with cloud ERP” on-demand.

 

The new ERP mindset is based on using standard processes everywhere you can

Another great SAP S/4HANA session was a panel discussion in which experts talked about “The new ERP mindset.” The mix of customer (Daniel Schumacher from Toyota Material Handling), partner (Rich Sernyak from PwC), and SAP (Eric van Rossum) vantage points provided a diverse perspective on what ERP means today. Maura Hameroff moderated.

The new ERP mindset means moving to standard processes and a more modular approach instead of using extensive customizations just because you can. Here are the key recommendations from the panelists”

  • PwC: define a clear business outcome, implement a scalable and agile platform to integrate new technologies such as AI, focus on predictability of time and cost.
  • Toyota: move from thinking about ERP for managing your current business to focusing on ERP as the platform to drive business transformation over next decade.
  • SAP: Focus on “opportunity readiness” to give your business the agility to adopt new business models and consume continuous innovation from SAP.

As both a customer of SAP S/4HANA and implementation partner, for a successful project, PwC also recommended considering which of your business processes are standard and which give you differentiated capabilities. For the former, use standard SAP; for the latter, follow clean core processes and extend your core system.

Toyota advised that you should not be afraid to disrupt your own business. Embrace disruptive technologies and try them out to understand the impact. One point Daniel made about the new ERP mindset is that what you used before successfully might not work for you tomorrow. He recommended having an open mind when you evaluate your processes. Fit to standard works in most cases. 80% of what most companies do is a commoditized process. Save your customization for what defines and differentiates your business.

Watch TRE321 “The new ERP mindset” on-demand.

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition roadmap session

Wow. This was a very fast-paced session, delivered by Arpan Shah, head of product management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. He shared planned innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that are likely to ship by the end of the year. Each topic included a short demo. Sorry if I missed any; they came pretty quickly. Usual roadmap disclaimer - all of these are planned and might not make it into the product.

  • My Home will make it easier access to the applications you use most with some AI-generated recommendations.
  • Microsoft Copilot integration brings the next step of collaborative ERP with easier access to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition through Microsoft Copilot.
  • Joule copilot makes your experience easier within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solution by offering context-based instructions of how to complete your work more effectively.
  • Easy filter lets you intuitively filter data in SAP Fiori elements list report applications using natural language.
  • Smart summarization makes information in object pages easier to share through email or the collaboration tool of your choice.
  • Financial business insights uses AI to discover what’s meaningful within selected review booklets.
  • A new version toggle for the Manage Journal Entries app simplifies change management for new apps by letting people try out new applications without risk and the ability to switch back to the original app.
  • Intelligent recommendations for advanced returns management reduces manual effort and facilitates faster returns order creation by prefilling fields using most common scenarios and history.
  • Intelligent project recommendations for employees give you higher confidence in matching employees with projects.
  • Hands-free production order management uses natural language through voice with Joule. This is useful in environments where there are typically no keyboards.
  • AI-driven back order processing execution lets you change prioritization through the Joule natural language interface.

Watch ERP127 “SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Find out what’s next” on-demand.

 

Arpan Shah preparing to run through a rapid-fire stream of planned innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

The ASUG Hub was a focal point of conversations about SAP S/4HANA migration

As the SAP Point of contact for user experience within ASUG, I spent much of my time in the ASUG Hub, speaking with customers about UX issues, generally in the context of moving to SAP S/4HANA or bringing the SAP Fiori user experience to a larger percentage of their organization.

ASUG hosted several customer roundtables and community alliance meet-ups where ASUG members could learn from peers and interact with SAP experts about how to generate more value from their investments in SAP. Todd Lutz, Michael Giessner, and Peter Keller hosted a great discussion on best practices for migrating to SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Mohammed Siddiqui, Cristin Charbonneau, and Todd Lutz held a live town hall to understand what issues are most important to the ASUG community.

It was great to meet so many old friends and people who I knew only from online encounters in various ASUG calls or the various user groups that I host for SAP Fiori developers or business analysts that want to adapt classic UI screens using SAP Screen Personas.

Thank you to everyone that attended my session, ASUG301 in the ASUG Hub. I talked about the state of SAP Fiori today, how we got here, and where we are going by adding AI to further accelerate application development and streamline the user experience. The audience was a good mix, some customers that are using SAP Fiori throughout their enterprise, many with some SAP Fiori deployed, and a few who have not yet started their SAP Fiori journey. If you missed it, you can download the slides.

My ASUG session covered the past, present, and future of SAP Fiori

 

Leadership lessons have a longer lifetime than technology

Another great session was a panel discussion on “Commonalities, not differences: Insights in military leadership.” Daria Wick moderated the conversation with Doug Melville, and a senior military leader. Melville is author of “Invisible Generals”, where he shares his family history that includes America’s first Black generals, father and son Benjamin O. Davis Sr. and Jr., who helped integrate America’s military and create the Tuskegee Airmen.

Peter with Doug Melville, author of Invisible Generals

The panelists talked about a variety of leadership topics including teamwork, empowerment, diversity, inclusion, and how to leave a lasting legacy. The recommendation to lead with inspiration rather than intimidation moved the conversation to some funny sports references about which players you would want to coach. The take away was even the difficult players can help a team win championships. Watch The Last Dance to see what they talked about.

In another session on inclusion, Julia White, SAP’s Chief Marketing and Solutions Officer,  Ada Agrait, SAP’s SVP and Global Head of Corporate Marketing, and Margot Goodson, VP, Head of North America Diversity & Inclusion, had a panel discussion about “Strengthening underrepresented communities: partnerships and best practices.” They talked about how to bring in under-represented groups to match your organization to the market it serves. Their top recommendations:

  • Julia: rely on diverse teams or risk bringing a product to market that serves the needs of only the people on the design team
  • Ada: follow the online conversations of the people you want to recruit so you learn what’s important to them and the language they use
  • Margot: Make DEI part of your DNA so you naturally recruit diverse and inclusive teams rather than having to fill gaps later

 

These were some highlights. My full list of UX and Application Development sessions at Sapphire 2024was in my preview blog post.

My colleague, Paul Saunders, wrote an excellent summary of Sapphire on LinkedIn.

It was also great to meet so many SAP colleagues in person for the first time, including Paul, Sinit, Daria, Christian, Marco, Eric, Maura, Sid, Mark, Sibylle, Margot, Tracy, Steve, and more (apologies if your name doesn’t appear).

 

We are taking the conversation deeper at our next SAP Fiori Innovation Day

If you want to go deeper on SAP Fiori and adjacent topics, we have our next SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago on Tuesday, August 13. Speakers from SAP, Mindset Consulting, Convergent IS, and Sovanta will cover what’s new in SAP’s UX portfolio, how to get started with SAP Fiori, AI innovations, the design process, and an optional hands-on workshop in which you will exercise all our tools and technologies to build an SAP Fiori app. Registration is now open.

On behalf of the SAP Cloud ERP UX Engineering team, Peter Spielvogel.

entry.title: Feature Request: Possibility to create a new Process Documentation Template in Excel format
entry.content:
Hello,

I'd like to suggest the creation of a documentation template in Excel format. Currently, I can only find the template to create a report based on a Word template.

SAP Signavio Process Manager

I am using a table fed by two blended live models where I the table option of Arrange totals doesn't work and also the right click on each dimension show totals is also greyed out? Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Replication Flow Blog Series Part 2 – Premium Outbound Integration
entry.content:
Hi @tobias_koebler,

I am trying to create a Replication Flow with Source S4HANA/CDS_Extraction/I_OUTBOUNDDELIVERY to AWS S3>ABC folder in csv format. When I do so, it creates another subfolder inside ABC folder, folder name as "I_OUTBOUNDDELIVERY" and places the CSV output. 

for ex: AWS S3>ABC>I_OUTBOUNDDELIVERY>part-systemgeneratedname.csv

Q.1) Is there a way that the CSV file creates directly under ABC Folder?

Q.2) Can I define the naming convention for the CSV file?

Kindly help.

Thank You!
VS

entry.title: Re: RAP Action: The command "COMMIT-ENTITIES" is not allo...

Diego is right. You must not perform COMMIT ENTITIES within the behavior implementation.
Therefore I marked it as solved.

entry.title: Re: How to customize the column list in the table setting...
entry.content:
@alina_be 

How about setting the UI.hidden: true annotation for the fields you do not want to expose to the user?

entry.title: SAP CQC Deployment Readiness for SAP Customer Data Cloud: New Updates for B2B
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

As you may know, the SAP Customer Qualification Check (CQC) for Deployment Readiness for SAP Customer Experience (CX) is a comprehensive service that analyzes your SAP Customer Experience tenant in detail to identify potential risks and ensure a smooth start of production. With the latest updates, we have expanded the scope of our checks to include B2B scenarios, providing you with even more assurance that your implementation will be successful.

In addition to our existing checks for B2C, we now offer the following checks for B2B: 

  • Activation of Organization Management: Ensure that organization management is activated for all needed sites. This can be configured in the B2C console under Administration > Organization Management.

  • Organization Self-Registration: Ensure that the organization self-registration screen-sets are customized using CSS styles and check the elements on the screens with their CSS classes. Verify that the styles reflect the company's corporate identity. You can find this in the B2C console under UI-Builder > Prefix-OrganizationRegistration.

  • Approval Workflows: Ensure that approval workflows are set up with at least one approver. Check that at least one approver with their email address is added. You can verify this by clicking the organization settings icon and selecting Approval Workflow. Here, at least one approval flow should be displayed.

  • Language-Related Settings: Check the elements for translation, including member attributes, communication templates, the delegated admin console, title, and settings. All elements should be translated for the languages in use.

  • Delegated Admin Settings: Check that the delegated admin portal is customized with a title, logo, settings, etc. The logo should be uploaded, the title should be set, and the other input fields should be maintained. You can check this by clicking the organization settings icon and selecting "Del Admin Titles & Settings" in the menu. Verify that valid values (company name, logo URL, etc.) are maintained.

  • Delegated Admin Look and Feel: Check the settings for the delegated admin look and feel. Validate that the colors are according to the company's style guidelines and that the potentially new delegated admin experience is activated. This ensures that customers accessing the delegated admin portal get the best experience with personalized environments.

  • Email Templates: Check that email templates are maintained with a valid sender name, display email (should not end with @gigya.com), subject, and email message. This ensures that end users get well-styled and formatted emails.
  • SMS Templates: Check that SMS templates are maintained with a custom SMS message. This ensures that end users get well-explaining SMS messages.

  • Policies: Check that the setup of policies is completed (check that details like policy ID, policy name, etc., are maintained). At least one policy should be defined with at least one asset defined.

  • Applications: Check the setup of applications so that at least one application is defined, including details like name (could be the company name), description, logo URL (should be the customer logo), and color indication (colors should match the customer's CI).

  • Assets: Verify that the asset types are set up with at least one type. For this, check that at least one attribute is created with the fields name, active, and type, and can be updated. Verify that for this asset type, at least one asset is defined.

  • Conditions: Verify that the conditions are set up with at least one type. For this, check that at least one attribute is created with the field name, active, type, and can be updated. Verify that for this asset type, at least one asset is defined.

 

How to request a CQC service?

  • Remote Services can be requested via the new Get Support application in SAP for Me (SAP Note 1296527). When submitting your request, please select: 1. Product: “Continuous Quality Check & Improvement Services” 2. Product function: “Service request for a Cloud Solution” If you need assistance in submitting your case, you may contact your local Customer Interaction Center (CIC).

 

Any Questions?

Please, contact the SAP Value Map team! 

Sign up for the SAP Enterprise Support value maps by requesting access to SAP Learning Hub or join the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps Learning Room if you already have access.

entry.title: Re: How to customize the column list in the table setting...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

either you do it via the annotation mentioned already @ui.hidden or you have a breakout in the frontend coding.

You get the SmartTable where you want to disable certain columns and you call the setIgnoredFields method:

 

 

var oSmartTable = this.getView().byId("<table_id>");
				oSmartTable.setIgnoredFields("<ColumnName>,<ColumnName2>,...");

 

 

More information here: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/api/sap.ui.comp.smarttable.SmartTable%23methods/setIgnoredFields

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Financials Development

entry.title: Re: SAP Sapphire 2024 highlights on UX, application development, and AI
entry.content: Great write up Peter!

entry.title: Blog Series: S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition – 01 Introduction & Supplier Delivery Prediction using AI
entry.content:
Introduction:

In addition to S/4 HANA On-premises/private cloud edition, SAP also offers SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition. Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) or Public Cloud Edition of SAP S/4 HANA, has high standardization and less flexibility to configure/modify. This has most of the pros and cons of a standard cloud solution. ‘The End of Corporate Computing’, Nicholas G. Carr, MIT Sloan Management Review & ‘How Software-as-a-Service Is Accelerating Digital Transformation’, Harvard Business Review are good articles that can provide a basic overview of Cloud Computing and SaaS.  

When to go for Public Cloud Edition?

System complexity and implementation size are important factors to be considered to determine the suitability of Public Cloud Edition. If the system complexity and implementation size are low, then cloud solutions should be evaluated. Since the cloud solutions can scale up faster than the on-premise solution, regardless of implementation size (with low complexity), cloud solutions can be considered. If the complexity of the implementation is high, then enhancements along with advanced functionalities may be required. Hence, the cloud solution may be difficult to cater to many complex business requirements. 

So, small to mid-size companies can consider public cloud options, since the business requirements are less complex, and the customization required is minimal (with limited integration). Subsidiaries are another area where public cloud solutions are relevant. SAP has released various documents related to the application of S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition for subsidiaries in a two-tier architecture. More details about this are available in the SAP Press E-Bite - SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Two-Tier ERP Landscapes.

There are differences in the functionalities available for On-Premise/Private Edition and Public Cloud Edition. Also, there is a common perception that S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition does not have some features that cater to complex business processes. However, more functionalities are introduced by SAP regularly. As of now, every year there are 2 major releases in February and August. Additionally, smaller fixes are released during these major releases. Systems are upgraded automatically during this release cycle. Please note that one of the major issues for most SAP on-premise customers is the high level of customization and inability to upgrade the system to the latest version. With this biannual automatic upgrade cycle and clear core concept with reasonable restrictions, this issue will be non-existent in the Public Cloud Edition. Moreover, Public Cloud Edition provides new features every year and customers can evaluate and activate it accordingly. Moreover, customers can use the ‘Customer Influence Portal’ to request SAP to develop new solutions that are not available in the roadmap.  

Hence, it is important to keep track of the available features in each release of Public Cloud Edition and frequently monitor the roadmap, so that companies can evaluate the pertinence of the solution to their business requirements. Interestingly, some of the new features are first released in the Public Cloud Edition before it is released in the on-premise or private edition. For example, in the Transportation Management area, the ability to use SAP TM Freight Order charges in the SAP SD billing document was first released in the Public Cloud Edition. Now this is available in on-premise / private edition too. Similarly, certain functionalities such as Freight settlement integration with FI/MM module are different in a Public Cloud Edition. In Public Cloud Edition the freight settlement is connected to lean service management (and not for the external services management) and SAP may or may not release this in the on-premise or private edition in the future.

This blog series will showcase some of the interesting solutions and important processes that can be achieved in S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition. Since SAP has detailed documentation for process flow, setup, and testing, these blogs will primarily focus on business problems, solutions/results, and business benefits. 

Business Requirement: Supplier Delivery Prediction

At the peak of the Gartner Hype cycle, Artificial Intelligence (AI) and its applications have great traction nowadays. So, I will start with an interesting AI/ML(Machine Learning) feature related to purchase order predicted delivery date in S/4 HANA Public Cloud. In general, when shippers/manufacturers order raw materials or semi-finished goods from vendors, it is usually difficult to estimate the accurate delivery date of the products at the receiving warehouse/plant. A delay in delivery can impact production and the availability of finished goods. This can in turn affect the outbound delivery schedule and customer satisfaction.  So, it is a common requirement to predict a realistic delivery date based on multiple factors and historical data using AI/ML. 

Solution:

In S/4 HANA public cloud, scope item 3FY (Prediction of Delivery Date for Purchase Order Items) deals with supplier delivery prediction. Here, Machine Learning algorithms identify the supplier delays based on the multiple situations and predict the chances of delay. It recognizes potential delivery delays and gains insights into the performance of the supplier based on past deliveries. This is an out-of-box functionality from SAP and the following steps are required. 

Intelligent Scenario Set-Up: 

Ensure the scope item is active, and the scenario SUPLRDELIVPREDICT is published and available. The scenario definition contains the input/output data parameters, model type, algorithm, and other details. 

Fiori App: Intelligent Scenarios

Train & activate the model: 

A large set of data is important to train the model. As per the documentation at least 1 year and/or 100k to 1 million records is the ideal volume of data. Also, data that is too old from a business perspective should be avoided. This means that customers who implemented AI features earlier will have more data in the system and the results/outputs from the AI model will be more accurate. The model still works with lower amount of data, but the accuracy may not be that precise. Once the model is trained, it can be activated. Only one model can be active at a time. We may have to re-train the model and the frequency can be determined as per the evaluation of the results. Scheduling options are also available in the app. 

Fiori App: Intelligent Scenario Management

Predict the delivery date:

Users can either use the Monitor Purchase Order Item App or the Scheduled Planned Tasks setting in the above Intelligent Scenario Management app to predict the delivery date for multiple purchase order items. Additionally, filters can be used to identify the orders that have potential delays, and users can follow up with the vendors or explore alternate sources of supply. In the beginning, this may be a manual process. But with Gen AI and RPA (Robotic Process Automation), these processes may be automated in the future. Other downstream processes such as notifications to carriers, customers, and other stakeholders, etc can also be automated.  

Fiori App: Monitor Purchase Order Items

 

Note: Refer to scope item 3FY for more details related to process flow, setup, and test scripts. (https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2308/SolP/3FY?region=US)

It is imperative to check whether the predictive delivery date is accurate. In the beginning, reports can be developed to check the accuracy of predicted results and compare them with the actual inbound receipt process (Please note that it will be a challenge to monitor the accuracy of predicted results in sandbox or development systems as the data may not be accurate). If we identify that the models are not predicting the expected results, using intelligent scenario management and BTP, advanced/custom models can be developed with the use of internal/external predictive analytics tools.  Otherwise, we can use SAP Customer Influence Portal or wait for SAP to release the updated version of the App. The important point to note is that architecture to predict delivery date in PO items is in place and there are multiple options to improve the results.   

Business Benefits:

  • Decrease manual effort to monitor and resolve issues earlier (proactively)
  • Plan and deliver materials more reliably.
  • Avoid production delay and rescheduling.
  • Get up-to-date lead time updates for goods/services at the master level for direct and indirect procurement.
  • Better visibility of supplier performance and inputs to streamline sources of supply.

Conclusion:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is a ready-to-run cloud ERP that delivers the latest industry best practices and continuous innovation. As of now, the target customers are those with low to medium system/process complexity. Every year, there are 2 major releases, and the system will be upgraded automatically. Hence the customers need not plan for long projects to upgrade the system and can activate the latest features released by SAP quickly.

One of the common business requirement of supplier delivery prediction using AI/ML is now an out-of-the-box solution in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition. Detailed documentation from SAP significantly helps to understand and implement the solution easily. Also, it is a bit impressive to see how quickly SAP is deploying functionalities based on the latest trends (AI in this case) in Public Cloud Edition and how easily customers can use it. Another advantage of the availability of this tool in S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition is the possibility of upgrade by SAP based on various self-learning techniques and additional parameters. If we need to use AI, it is important to start early and adopt the standard SAP architecture earlier than later. There will be significant advantages for being the first mover. 

SAP has not fully released the Gen-AI capabilities via SAP Joule and it will be interesting to evaluate various use cases and business requirements/solutions available for end customers and implement it. Considering all these, it is critical for most of the customers to evaluate whether SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition with its current scope and future roadmap is suitable for their business processes and keep monitoring the evolution of the product.

Link to other SAP blogs: https://community.sap.com/t5/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/179579

entry.title: SAP SF Employee Central Core Question
entry.content:
Hello Friends !

Please suggest your answer to the below question.

How do you create country specific field for the Legal Entity Object?
Please choose one correct answer:

a) As a generic object with a Valid When association to the Legal Entity object.
b) As an HRIS element in the Corporate Data Model with a Composite association to the Legal Entity object.
c) As an HRIS element in the Country Specific Field for Corporate Data Model with a Valid When Association to the Legal Entity object.

d) As a generic object with a composite association to the Legal Entity object.

#SAP Employee Central Core,
SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central 
#Employee central core
#SAP SF EC
#THR81

entry.title: Re: item code being replace to nearest item from item mas...

Hi EunSeok,

I have tested for copy table it will not work. If user key in manually, the suggestion will prompt.

Thank you.

Best regards,

Tan Su Hsiang

entry.title: Apply filter on data reading via multiple sources
entry.content:
Hello Everyone 

I have created an entity set in SAP CAP which has few columns from on-premise entity set and few from HANA cloud table.

Sample Entity Set

 

entity MyEntity       as
    select from onpremise.Entity as a inner join db.AnotherTable as b
 on a.pk = b.fpk {
      key a.pk as key,
        a.p1 as p1,
        a.p2 as p2,
        b.p3 as p3,
        b.p4 as p4
}

Property pf,p1 and p2 from on-premise entity service but p3 and p4 from HANA cloud . HANA cloud Table is created in db folder.

I have successfully read the data using SAP cloud SDK from on-premise and cds.ql from hana cloud . 

If UI passes $filter like p1=‘v1’ and p3=‘v2' (mix of property from on-premise and hana cloud). How I can split the filter in better way so that I can use respective filter while reading from on-premise and hana cloud .

Is there any generic way to handle $filter,$top and $skip. Are there any specific cds method I need to use to perform the filter and other odata operations

Kindly guide me

Thanks

Sandeep

entry.title: How to integrate with SAP using Odata and BAPIs
entry.content:
Hi Team,                                      

Objective:

Vistaar has a solution which accepts sales transaction data, processes it and generates recommendations for customers. Our solution is built using client and server-side JavaScript and is hosted in AWS cloud.                                           

We have checked out SAP Business Accelerator Hub for published Odata SAP APIs. We also evaluated Try-out using SAP's sandbox environment. We would like to get further help on Integration:

  1. Besides OData APIs, are there standard BAPIs in SAP ECC and S/4 PERSON_NAME systems that can be used for integration, such as fetching data from SAP? Also how can they be consumed in an external Java-based application?
  2. We generate price recommendations (Pricing Records, Conditions) and need to know which SAP OData objects/Odata APIs/BAPIs to use to send these recommendations to SAP.
  3. How can we read data incrementally from SAP system?
  4. How can we write data in bulk to SAP system?
  5. Right now, the sales transaction data is spread across multiple SAP objects, and we need to use "expand" syntax of OData to read these multiple objects. Do you offer another interface where we can fetch the sales transaction data as a set of records?

Please guide me on how to go about achieving the above.                      
                             


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to integrate with SAP using Odata and BAPIs
entry.content: Open this query on behalf of customer, seek helps from community experts.

entry.title: Re: UOM KG TO NOS Items per unit decimal 6 digits Issue i...
entry.content:
Hello Naresh,

 

This is by system definition.

you set the decimal places for Qty to 5 in General settings, and B1 converted the Qty. for inventory UoM accordingly by ROUND(212*0.018519,5) = 3.92603.

 

Rounding it to 4 by manual input or UDV or whatever will cause the change of Purchase quantity  because Purchase Qty. and Inv UoM Qty. are bi directional by UoM conversion calculation.

 

The current behavior is logically right, however if you want to have a capability to overwrite the inventory UoM without affecting Purchase Qty., I think you need to request it to customer influence. But this is a long term solution and cannot be applicable to the customer right now.

 

If you really want to implement it as you want, I can think of the following dirty solution.

  1. new Quantity UDF to keep the purchase qty unchanged
  2. UDV to replicate the UDF to original Qty field.
  3. UDV to round off Qty(Inventory UoM) to integer
    1. original Qty field will be changed too

 

I’ve not tested it. If this doesn’t work, you need to go with UI API.

 

Regards
EunSeok

entry.title: early watch alertの停止方法
entry.content:
early watch alertの停止方法を教えていただきたく、よろしくお願いいたします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: early watch alertの停止方法
entry.content:
SOLMAN_SETUP -> Application Operations -> EarlyWatch Alert Management -> Activate EWA ->uncheck "Activate"

お手数ですが、t-code:SOLMAN_SETUPを使用していただき、Application Operations -> EarlyWatch Alert Management ->該当システムを選択 -> Activate EWAのステップ->編集モードで有効化を取り消し

ケースの中に画面キャプチャをご参照ください。

entry.title: SolutionManager7.2 SP18のインストール
entry.content:
SolutionManager7.2　SP18の初期設定ガイド(SOLMAN SETUP)を確認できずにおります。
こちらあるかご教示ください。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SolutionManager7.2 SP18のインストール
entry.content:
SolutionManager7.2　SP18の関連ガイドは以下をご参照ください。
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager?locale=en-US

SolutionManager7.2　SP18の日本語ガイドはありませんが、以下をご参照すればよいです。設定中に何か問題がなければ、ケースをオープンすれば対応させていただきます。
https://support.sap.com/ja/alm/solution-manager/knowledge-transfer.html
＝＞SAP Solution Manager 7.2 初期設定ガイド (SPS9)

entry.title: Is it really impossible to assign the same sales group to multiple sales offices in SAP CBC?
entry.content:
Hello!

I would like to ask a question regarding Sales offices and Sales groups in SAP CBC.

We are currently working on completing the organisational structure in CBC and faced with an issue that we are not able to assign the same sales group to multiple sales offices.

We would like to know if it is really impossible as multiple assignment is needed for our case.

Best Regards,

Fujita

entry.title: How to register Bank Account ID

There is no "Bank Account ID" in the API settings. Please tell me how to set it.

This is the API information in question.

DebtorItem | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP GUI for HTML (WebGUI) SSO using Kerberos - Connecting to Multiple SIDs at the Same Time
entry.content:
Hello,

We have been running a successful deployment of SAP Secure Login Client 3.0 for many years that provides Kerberos based SSO via the SAP GUI to our backend SAP NetWeaver 7.50 based systems.

Recently I have looking at SAP SSO via SAP GUI for HTML (WebGUI) and have found a few peculiar behaviors.

I am only able to SSO via WebGUI to a single SID at any one time.  If I open an additional browser tab and attempt to SSO to another SID, I always get the logon screen.  If you close the browser and attempt to connect to the second SID it will connect and SSO with no problems.

SID 1 = DEV // SID 2 = TEST // SID 3 = QA

So if I open Edge and SSO to DEV it will work.  Open a new tab and attempt to connect to TEST or QA and I will get the logon screens for both SIDs.  Close the browser and reopen, and not connect to TEST it will work.  Open additional tabs trying to connect to DEV and QA, you get the logon screens.

It's like the browser can only handle a single SAP Kerberos session at one time.

Can anyone help with this behavior?

Cheers

Shaun

entry.title: Re: 无鸟用的SAP PA证书，刚入行的同行可以考一考
entry.content:
赞同。

干了15年了，PA认证让个人对系统有比较全面和系统性认识，在实际项目中PA证书商务层面可作为加分项，但一个明智的甲方会要求经验优先PA认证。

因此不建议花大价钱为了PA认证去自费参考培训，通常都很贵。另外接触的微软认证大同小异。

entry.title: SAP FE ListReport to filter list to show newly created entity after Action to create new entity.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have CAP Application which has a List report FE application. We have unbound action that lets the user create a NEW entity in a dialog. Once we create the new entity successfully, we need the List report to filter and just show this new Entry.

We have some standard filters and we could use to filter the list.

We need help on what needs to be implemented to allow us to filter the list once we confirm okay on a messagebox afte successfully creating this record.

Any help much appreciated.

Cheers

DC

Sam

Sammar

entry.title: Handling Item that cannot be splited in Putaway Roll
entry.content:
Hi Masters

    I want to ask what is the best practice for handling item that have characteristic by roll example like fabric, the requirement is that we do not want to cut the fabric so putaway must be done on 1 roll but each roll is unique and can consists of different qty example same article will have 200m 210m or maybe 194m ( from vendors, PO uom is in meters ).  How we plan to putaway those item ? regarding capacity check ? right now SAP will split by palletization and that is not accurate... and it required user to check first if the bin is enough to store 1 roll of item  

Thank you for your input

entry.title: Re: Migration from classic story to Optimize Story
entry.content: There is no such mass migration tool right now.

entry.title: Dunning Adobe form Custom Validations
entry.content:

I am trying to implement ADOBE form for dunning process to generate dunning letter.

but before printing I need some validations for Customer. I am not getting the exact path where to put these validations. FIBF(00001720) fi-fi changed to FI_PRINT_DUNNING_NOTICE_PDF. But I am not getting where to put validations to avoid creating dunning if any validation fails.

Kindly help me with any approach I should follow.

I am new to FI, BTE world.

 

Thanks in advance,

Abhishek Aggarwal

entry.title: ISC - Error response received from destination endpoint. ErrorMessage: I/O error: Read timed out
entry.content:
Hi Expert(s),

We have configured an Intelligent Service Centre event connector for hire event to call a CPI program to auto create absences and as expected when there are new hire(s) absences are created as per the dates maintained in the look up table SF EC.

Issue we are facing is when we check the status of the process in execution manage dashboard it is still in "In Progress" and error log says "Error response received from destination endpoint. ErrorMessage: I/O error: Read timed out"

can someone please help here what might be the issue and how to resolve this.

Thanks,

Sriram

Then, users cannot create outbaound delivery, but sales order can be saved.
I would like to know how to make it impossible to save the sales order.

entry.title: Re: Not possible to display SAC Live BW Query Texts in Ge...
entry.content: If you have maintained text in BW and then set data access language in user preference, it works in SAC.

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hi,

I am excited to try out this new challenge. Thanks for a very detailed step-by-step instructions.

I am receiving an error in Step 9. I have created DevChallenger user and executed GRANT statement. No errors. I was able to complete sub-step 1 under Step 9 which is - Store the credentials of the DevChallenger user in the HANA Client user store similarly to what you did for the user DBAdmin, but now using the key myDevChallenger.

I am unsure about what I missed. Any guidance will be appreciated.

Thanks in advance,

Regards

ASM

entry.title: Streamline Customer Return Management with SAP Business AI
entry.content:
The rise of Artificial Intelligence (AI) has revolutionized traditional business operations, replacing mundane tasks with intelligent automation. One such exciting application is in the domain of customer returns in SAP S/4HANA, where AI modulation creates strategic recommendations to optimize business processes and support revenue growth. Of such applications, SAP S/4Hana Cloud's contribution is notable, especially with its public edition. This AI-infused technology constructs a dynamic customer return recommendation model that streamlines businesses, enhancing customer experience and satisfaction.

From a Process improvement perspective, Integrating AI into customer returns enhances the efficiency and effectiveness of business processes. It automates data analysis, pre-empts return reasons, and recommends the most suitable action. Moreover, AI fosters process standardization, eliminating inconsistencies and reducing process deviations. This automation allows the customer service team adequate time to refine their core competencies and focus on higher-value tasks, ultimately improving productivity.

In this scenario, Stella, the sales representative is tasked with creating a return order for a customer. She is utilizing Joule, SAP's generative AI copilot, to comprehend creating a return order. Promptly inputting her information, Stella selects return recommendations provided by Joule. Upon completion of the return order, Stella shares this information with her colleagues via Microsoft Teams to ensure the swift and thorough execution of the return order to meet the customer's expectations.

Let's look at a step-by-step method -

Fig 1 : User Log in page with Joule

 

Stella logs in to the system. She clicks on the diamond icon to activate Joule, a new-generation digital assistant copilot, to investigate her current task of handling a customer return request.

Fig 2: Interacting with Joule

Stella received a request from a customer to return items that were delivered to them recently. To ensure a smooth return process for the customer, Stella turns to her AI copilot Joule for real-time guidance on handling return orders. Based on the options provided by Joule, Stella clicks on the "Manage Customer Return: version 2" app to create a return order for the customer.

Fig 3 : Return Order creation

The system opens the app and allows Stella to fill necessary inputs for processing.

Fig 4: Return Order

She quickly fills in item details for the return order.

Fig 5: AI based Return Recommendation

With the help of AI, the system promptly generates a "Return Recommendation" for the customer. Stella then chooses from the list of recommendations and moves on to the next step.

Fig 6 : Return Order for Item 2

Stella realizes that the same customer is impacted by a product recall initiated by her company due to a product quality issue. She decides to handle both return orders at the same time.

Fig 7 : AI based Return Recommendation for Item 2

Stella finally selects the recommendation for the product that has been recalled by her company.

Fig 8 : Return Recommendation for both materials

Stella accepts the Recommendation and saves. Stella has now created 2 return orders on the way with minimal effort thanks to AI, and the customer will be happy with the best possible service from the vendor.​

Fig 9 : Microsoft teams Integration

Newly created return orders can be shared easily in Microsoft Teams with respective Colleagues to initiate order-related discussion if it is required.

Fig 10 : Microsoft Teams : Message

With this Microsoft Teams integration, respective colleagues will get details of newly created return orders directly in their Microsoft Teams.

Customer Benefits :

With AI-powered return recommendations, customers receive a swift and simpler return process, which directly enhances experience and builds loyalty towards the brand. By integrating AI in return processes on SAP S/4Hana Cloud, businesses could potentially reduce return registrations significantly. Customers can obtain immediate responses and update the status of their returns without the need for manual intervention. This speeds up the overall return process, thereby ensuring customer satisfaction.

The intelligent system also recommends alternative solutions based on the customer's historical data regarding preferences and behavior. This personalization of services based on customer past behavior incites a sense of recognition and increases brand preference, resulting in customer delight.

Moreover, the seamless integration of multiple platforms allows customers from various touchpoints to effortlessly track their return status and receive faster solutions. The transparency this system offers drives trust, and trust is the cornerstone of any business-consumer relationship.

In conclusion, integrating AI into customer return processes on SAP S/4HANA Cloud significantly accelerates the overall return process, resulting in a range of benefits for customers including:

  • Improved Efficiency: AI-driven automation enables businesses to streamline sales operations, reduce manual effort, and minimize errors. This allows the customer service team to focus on higher-value tasks, leading to increased productivity.
  • Enhanced Customer Satisfaction: Customers benefit from immediate responses and real-time updates on the status of their returns, resulting in a quicker and more efficient return process, ultimately leading to higher levels of customer satisfaction.

 

For more information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, check out the following links:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud release info here
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Early Release Series Webinars here
  • Latest 2402 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release Blogs here
  • Product videos on our SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA YouTube playlist
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition Early Release Series here
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for User Experience and Cross Topics, Public Edition Continuous Influence Session here
  • Inside SAP S/4HANA Podcast here
  • openSAP microlearning for SAP S/4HANA here
  • Inside SAP S/4HANA Podcast here
  • Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud here
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Community: here
  • Feature Scope Description here
  • What’s New here
  • Help Portal Product Page here
  • Implementation Portal here

 

Please Check out my latest Blogs on SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

 

All AI Blogs :

  • Harnessing Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA Sales Order Extraction from PDF
  • Optimizing Procurement Processes with Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP S/4HANA Central Invoice Ma...

entry.title: Re: Lead & Lag : SQL Window Functions and a bit more ...
entry.content: @MioYasutake  - Thank you for your feedback!

entry.title: Re: Line Approval or rejection by Approving Authorities
entry.content:
Hi shekharsharma 

Unfortunately, in the current B1, the approval process triggers only when the conditions of certain approval template are met. So you can take a look at the template -Terms tab. 

It is not the case that user can select some rows to trigger the process, instead, you can design your own approval query to define a condition those rows might meet, such as a certain value of document total or conditions like that.

If you want to raise a special function request for the current approval process, please follow the attached note 1028874 to submit details.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh

entry.title: Missing packages for S/4 Migration in Maintenance Planner
entry.content:
Hello friends.

When I upload my XML in the Maintenance Planner and plan an S4 migration, I get the message that packages are missing. How do I proceed now? Do I have to wait until the next release?

Missing packages:

SAPK-10802INS4CEXT
SAPK-10802INS4CORE
SAPK-10802INS4COREOP
SAPK-10802INS4DEPREC
SAPK-10802INS4FND

entry.title: Re: OLE Error : Upload Excel file without having Office i...
entry.content:
Try with Class CL_FDT_XL_SPREADSHEET. 

To my knowledge this independent of Office installation. And pretty sinmple to use

entry.title: Re: Spool request not generated for custom form in EWM
entry.content:
The transaction for maintaining condition records related to Physical Inventory printing should be /SCWM/PRPI_GCM.

Application - PPI

Maintenance Group - PI

Maintenance Context - GCM

entry.title: Re: Not able to maintain condition record for custom phys...
entry.content:
The transaction for maintaining condition records related to Physical Inventory printing should be /SCWM/PRPI_GCM.

Application - PPI

Maintenance Group - PI

Maintenance Context - GCM

entry.title: Re: Migration from classic story to Optimize Story
entry.content:
Hello @basavarajRoddanavar

can you let me know if it is mandatory to migrate all the classic stories to ODE until end of the year?

Is there an official communication vom SAP to the SAC tenant owners?

Thanks & best regards, Martin

entry.title: What is the Better way for developing Reports in SAP S/4 Hana private Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts!

We are working on SAP S/4 Hana Private Edition implementation, it would be grateful  if you could suggest what is the better way for developing Reports in Private Edition?

Whether we need to develop in ALV Reports in SE38 or any other feasible way for developing in Private edition?

Thanks in Advance,

Sai Bhargav

entry.title: Re: insufficient privilege currency conversion to shared...
entry.content:
Hi @DerekJ 

I have had no problem. I'm not sure how you shared the views, but it's not done in the usual way.

My setup is:

  • The source space: In this space we creae a connection to SAP ERP or BW, and then import the tables using create object (+) "Currrency Conversion Views". Select the SAP connection and this will import all the necessary. Then share them with other spaces using the specific section.



  • The consumer spaces: in the spaces that you want to use the conversion data, you must recreate the process again, with the option of (+) “Currrency Conversion Views”, but as a source, instead of using a connection, you select the space from where you have shared the currency information.
    Create the views and you can use them.

You can use any view that does currency conversion in both DSP and HANA DB space.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Migration from classic story to Optimize Story
entry.content:
Hi MKretlein, please go through the below blog for detailed timelines about migration

Important Notice: Changes in SAP Analytics Cloud C... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Project Stock in production order
entry.content:
hi @AdelaKov ,

to achieve this, the field Individual/collective in the material master (MRP) should have the indicator "Only collective requirements" for your lower BOM level components, 

This indicator, on the MRP view in the standard system, determines whether individual and/or collective requirements are allowed for the dependent requirements generated by the material.

1 = individual requirement (sales order/project stock) = your finished goods level

2 = collective requirement = lower BOM level components

Blank = inherits upper level materials' behaviour value which created the dependent requirement for the component

entry.title: How to Develop Adobe Forms in SAP S/4 Private Edition like Public Edition through Binding Fields?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts!

Is it feasible to develop Adobe forms in SAP S/4 Hana Private Edition through Binding Fields(XML) similar to how we do in SAP S/4 Hana Public Edition?

It would be grateful if you could help me with steps needs to be followed for development of Adobe Forms in Private Edition.

Thanks,

Sai Bhargav

entry.title: Re: SAP ERP – PP61 T-Code – Managerial control for relate...
entry.content: Is there a different cloud solution that works integrated with SAP or an alternative standard tool in SAP SuccessFactors Platform or SAP Fiori for SAP ERP HCM that will meet this need (Shift Planning)?

entry.title: Re: Line Approval or rejection by Approving Authorities
entry.content:
Hi Nidhi,

The requirement is little bit different. While an approver is approving the document he should able to approve partial line items.

For Example, One Document is having 5 line items and approver wants to approve only 2 line items.

entry.title: Re: Messages in SAC Handling with BW Live Connection
entry.content: Thank you for your answer. Regarding the first idea, I haven't found any script so far that can visualize or retrieve the system messages. Only system-level traces can be retrieved, but these are system models like ERROR_LOG. As for the second idea of connecting to the BW system, I initially saw it as somewhat complicated to do, but I will look into it. Thanks!

entry.title: What is dump DBIF_NTAB_TABLE_NOT_FOUND?
entry.content:
Hi,

I've got a dump which is DBIF_NTAB_TABLE_NOT_FOUND. The error analysis is like below: Structure description "XXXX" was not found. In the event of a missing structure description for an include, this error may have occurred while accessing a structure or a table in which this include is used.

This error happened in an interface method calling. Does anyone explain more about this dump?

Regards,

Eric

entry.title: Re: How to display custom data in the custom section of f...
entry.content: I've created a section, and using odata v4, there is one large binary data in the api, that is not getting displayed in that section

entry.title: Re: How to display custom data in the custom section of f...
entry.content:
I've created a section, and using v4 service, there is one large binary data in the service, that is not getting displayed in that section by direct binding, it is showing path of that large binary data, now somehow i want to read that data and want to display it over section.

<core:FragmentDefinition
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:macros="sap.fe.macros"
    xmlns:code="sap.ui.codeeditor"
>
    <code:CodeEditor
        id="_IDGenCodeEditor1"
        core:require="{ handler: '.....'}"
        type="xml"
        value="{model>/data}"
        editable="false"
        height="100%"
        width="100%"
        maxLines="100000"
    />
</core:FragmentDefinition>

entry.title: Re: Basics of Nodejs and Expressjs in SAP BAS
entry.content: What does this article have to do with Business Application Studio or SAP? Moreover, it is not aligned with the SAP best practices of application development, which are outlined in the SAP BTP Developer's Guide linked here: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-guidance-framework/guidance-framework/what-is-sap-btp-guidance-framework

entry.title: Re: Purchasing unit not being respected when converting P...
entry.content:
If a conversion from PPDS is triggered then it is done with the old logic so with ME21 and not the enjoy version.
There is a difference. Have you checked if you convert the order in ECC /S4 with the old logic I guess you will get the same result in that as you perform the conversion from PPDS.

If this is the same result as via PPDS then I think there is a way to "switch" the the new conversion logic in PPDS during CIF to ECC and S4. See note 1737155 Using new me21n enjoy functionality for PO from APO.

entry.title: Hi, I received a file update from Descartes that a few tariff numbers are changed. We have uploade
entry.content:
We have received a notification from Descartes that some tariff codes are changed. We have uploaded the xml file and tried to reclassify the products.

When we copy data, we get this message: " 7219310010 Consigned from Taiwan or Vietnam". The new code is uploaded in the system, but what that this message actually mean?
